author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: DeepSource ruleset alignment: tune .deepsource.toml so reported issues reflect repo policy (lands before all category triage)

Top rules by occurrence enumerated (authenticated dashboard/API) and dispositioned rule-by-rule: conflicts with repo idioms (TS-first patterns, docstring gate scope, intentional CLI process.exit etc.) get skipcq/toml suppressions WITH written rationale; genuinely useful rules stay enabled; post-alignment counts recorded on pm-vlnc; no blanket category disables without rationale

message: The 7.2k anti-pattern + 2.8k documentation stock strongly suggests default-JS-ruleset mismatch against a strict-TS codebase that already enforces 100% coverage, docstrings, jscpd, and 17-max ESLint suppressions. Aligning the analyzer beats 10k mechanical edits. DeepSource free tier supports rule disabling via .deepsource.toml and inline skipcq. This child BLOCKS meaningful counts for the category children — do it first.
